steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize Malta's deadweight tonnage. To emphasize a number, the best approach is to compare it to a reference point that makes it look large. The notes give two comparisons: Taiwan's average DWT (17,000 tons) and the global average DWT (21,389 tons). Malta's average is 54,000 tons — well above both.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In 2021, Malta's merchant ships had an average deadweight tonnage of 54,000 tons, higher than the global average of 21,389 tons.: States Malta's DWT and contrasts it with the global average, directly emphasizing how large Malta's figure is. ✓
  • Choice B — Taiwan had 429 merchant ships in 2021, while Malta had 2,137.: Compares ship counts, not deadweight tonnage. ✗
  • Choice C — In 2021, Taiwan's 429 merchant ships had an average deadweight tonnage of 17,000 tons.: Focuses on Taiwan's DWT; Malta isn't mentioned at all. ✗
  • Choice D — The average carrying capacity, or deadweight tonnage, of merchant ships in Taiwan was 17,000 tons in 2021.: Again only discusses Taiwan; omits Malta entirely. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
